title: IAM Least Privilege — password policy, role scoping, access analyzer, MFA
description: An engineer is standing up IAM baselines for a new AWS account: password policy, Access Analyzer, role scoping for IaC and application workloads, MFA enforcement, and the `hulumi:iac-role=true` tag convention that the drift classifier depends on. Target end state matches Hulumi's `AccountFoundation.iamBaseline` — no inline admin policies, no wildcard `Resource: '*'` outside `iam:List*` / `iam:Get*` reads, Access Analyzer archive rules for Hulumi-tagged principals.
actors: External Attacker (via stolen IAM access key)
Compromised CI runner (semi-trusted, could exfil if over-scoped)
assets: IAM user passwords and access keys
IAM roles and their assume-role trust policies

stride.name: Wildcard IAM policy elevation
stride.description: An LLM-authored or human-authored policy grants `Action: '*' / Resource: '*'` to a workload role. A compromised workload becomes effectively admin. Hulumi's HardeningPack refuses such policies at preview time outside narrow `iam:*` read namespaces.

stride.name: Assume-role trust spoofing
stride.description: An IAM role trust policy allows `Principal: '*'` (or a too-broad principal set) to assume it. External account principals can escalate via assume-role chains. Mitigation: explicit principal ARNs, `sts:ExternalId` conditions for cross-account.

stride.name: Untagged IaC role breaks attribution
stride.description: If the IaC deploy role lacks `hulumi:iac-role=true`, the drift classifier's CloudTrailAdapter cannot distinguish IaC-originated from human-console changes, corrupting every subsequent drift verdict. Tag was advisory through M2–M4 and is mandatory at v1.0.0 (paired with the SCP template shipped in M5).

stride.name: Tag tampering on the IaC role
stride.description: A principal with `iam:TagRole` can add `hulumi:iac-role=true` to an attacker-controlled role, making their CloudTrail events look like IaC. The v1.0.0 SCP template (M5) denies `iam:TagRole` with that key from non-IaC principals.

stride.name: Access Analyzer disabled — external access blind spot
stride.description: Without Access Analyzer, external-access findings on roles, buckets, KMS keys, and Secrets Manager are invisible. Sandbox tier is advisory; Startup-Hardened enables Access Analyzer in account-local or organization mode.

stride.name: Agent-driven credential brute force
stride.description: AI-agent-driven attacks can automate credential-stuffing against IAM users. MFA enforcement + IAM password policy (14+ chars, complexity, rotation) + IA-5 authenticator management are the baseline.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.baseline.aws.AccountFoundation.iamBaseline
recommendedComponents.rationale: Password policy (CIS §1.6/1.8/1.9), Access Analyzer (Startup-Hardened, CIS §1.19), archive rules for Hulumi-tagged principals, no inline admin.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.policies.aws.HulumiHardeningPack (H3 rule: iac-role tag present)
recommendedComponents.availability: Shipped in M2 advisory; mandatory at v1.0 (M5) + SCP-paired
recommendedComponents.rationale: Detects missing `hulumi:iac-role=true` tag at preview time. SCP template `docs/deployment/scp.json` (shipped in M5) enforces tag-integrity server-side.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.policies.aws.CisV5Pack (section 1 — IAM)
recommendedComponents.rationale: CIS v5.0 section-1 rules enforce password policy, MFA, credential rotation, and wildcard-scoping preventive checks.
